question_text: A student has a piece of metal with a mass of 270 g. When placed in a measuring cylinder of water, the water level rises from 100 cm³ to 130 cm³. Calculate the density of the metal in g/cm³, and predict whether it would float or sink in water (density of water = 1 g/cm³).
correct_answer: Volume of metal = 130 - 100 = 30 cm³. Density = mass ÷ volume = 270 ÷ 30 = 9 g/cm³. The metal will sink because 9 g/cm³ is much greater than 1 g/cm³ (water's density).
explanation: This multi-step problem requires students to first find the volume by calculating the displacement, then use the density formula, and finally apply the floating/sinking rule. It tests calculation skills and conceptual understanding together.
